content: We can model an open-ended Task and Motion Planning (TAMP) problem for a robotic agent as a dynamic unified graph, where unseen everyday objects are novel zero-shot nodes that the system must generalize over without additional fine-tuning. Curiosity-driven numerical operator learning from hybrid planning-learning pipelines can be repurposed to automatically generate semantic and spatial embeddings for these new unseen object nodes, enabling zero-shot generalization to unforeseen manipulation tasks in novel environments.
source_topics: Zero-shot node generalization on unified graph
trigger_topic: Zero-shot node generalization on unified graph
